description: Eval cases for code quality: dead code, naming, DRY principle, documentation

cases.name: Unreachable code path
Unclear variable names
cases.description: Variables with cryptic or misleading names

cases.name: Repeated code blocks
cases.description: Duplicated logic that should be extracted
cases.code_snippet:
class UserService:
    def create_admin_user(self, email, password):
        user = User(email=email, password=hash_password(password))
        user.role = 'admin'
        user.created_at = datetime.now()
        user.is_active = True
        db.save(user)
        return user
    
    def create_regular_user(self, email, password):
        user = User(email=email, password=hash_password(password))
        user.role = 'user'
        user.created_at = datetime.now()
        user.is_active = True
        db.save(user)
        return user

cases.name: Missing function documentation
cases.description: Complex function without docstring or comments

cases.name: Overly complex function
cases.description: Function with too many branches and conditions

cases.name: Well-structured, documented code
cases.code_snippet:
def calculate_discount(order: Order) -> float:
    """Calculate total discount for an order based on customer tier and promotions.
    
    Args:
        order: Order object with customer and promo_code
    
    Returns:
        Discount amount as float
    """
    tier_discount = _get_tier_discount(order.customer)
    promo_discount = _get_promo_discount(order.promo_code) if order.promo_code else 0
    return tier_discount + promo_discount

def _get_tier_discount(customer: Customer) -> float:
    """Get discount percentage based on customer tier."""
    tier_discounts = {
        'vip': 0.2,
        'member': 0.1,
        'standard': 0.0
    }
    return customer.total_spend * tier_discounts.get(customer.tier, 0)
